text:
In the quiet town of Willow Creek lived a young boy named Leo. Every day after school, Leo would race along the winding trails through the woods. On one particularly warm summer day, he found a forgotten relic: an antique compass.

Curious about ancient things, Leo took it home. Late one night, after a secret adventure exploring nearby caves, he noticed the compass spinning. It pointed him towards an old house with a cracked window.

That morning, Leo visited the house. It was quiet—a relic itself. Inside, he discovered a dusty photo album filled with pictures of a family that seemed magical. They all had those big, curious eyes, and one looked remarkably like Leo.

Encouraged by this discovery, Leo started reading. The album told of a legendary hero named Luna whom it claimed had once lived there. Luna had a pet phoenix. Every night, Luna would summon the phoenix to release a song that guided lost travelers.
